prerequisites: A Chameleon account with access to the token settings page at https://app.chameleon.io/settings/tokens.
An account-specific secret token generated in Chameleon, which the connector supplies as the required `api_key` field. Tokens can only be downloaded once, so store the value securely when generated.
A `start_date` value (required) marking the earliest point from which the connector reads data.

authMethods.name: API key (account secret token)
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates with a single secret field, `api_key`, which holds your Chameleon account-specific secret token. Chameleon authenticates each REST API request (base URL `api.chameleon.io`, served over HTTPS) with the `X-Account-Secret` header or an `account_secret` parameter. Generate the token at https://app.chameleon.io/settings/tokens; it is account-specific and can only be downloaded once, so keep it secure.

setupSteps.title: Generate your Chameleon account secret token
setupSteps.body: Sign in to Chameleon and open the token settings at https://app.chameleon.io/settings/tokens. Generate an account-specific secret token. Because the token can only be downloaded once, copy and store it immediately in a secure location.

setupSteps.title: Provide the token as the api_key
setupSteps.body: Enter the generated secret token into the connector's required `api_key` field. Chameleon uses this value as the `X-Account-Secret` header (or `account_secret` parameter) to authenticate REST API requests against `api.chameleon.io`.

setupSteps.title: Set the sync window and optional filters
setupSteps.body: Set the required `start_date` to control the earliest data the connector reads. Optionally set `end_date` to bound the latest data, `filter` to restrict the records returned, and `limit` to cap the number of records per request.

setupSteps.title: Account for API rate limits
setupSteps.body: Chameleon enforces a global rate limit of roughly 60 to 120 requests per minute across all endpoints, plus concurrency limits on some endpoints. Rate-limited responses return headers such as `X-Retry-After`, `X-Ratelimit-Limit`, or `X-Ratelimit-Wait` indicating how long to wait before retrying.
